summary.intent: Improve model sorting in the onboarding and settings pages so that models without createdAt dates (like Codex models) are sorted by version number (highest first) instead of alphabetically.
summary.outcome: Added a shared `modelVersionKey` helper to helpers.js and updated sort logic in onboarding-view.js, page-providers.js, and providers.js to use version number extraction as a fallback when no createdAt date is available.

repo: Model sort logic is duplicated across three files: onboarding-view.js, page-providers.js, and providers.js — all three needed updating for consistent behavior.
All three JS files import from helpers.js, making it the right place for shared utility functions like model sorting helpers.
Some providers (e.g. Codex/OAuth) do not populate createdAt on model objects, causing alphabetical sort to produce unintuitive ordering (GPT 5 before GPT 5.4).

code.finding: New modelVersionKey helper added here to extract numeric version from model IDs for sort fallback.
Sort function updated to use modelVersionKey fallback when createdAt is absent.
Sort function updated to use modelVersionKey fallback when createdAt is absent.
Has its own independent sort for the preferred models modal; also updated to use modelVersionKey fallback.

workflow: Grep for existing export functions and import patterns before deciding where to add shared utilities.
Check all files with similar sort logic when making a sorting change — it was spread across 3 files, not just the initially targeted 2.

friction: Model sort logic was not centralized, requiring the same change to be applied in three separate files.
No existing shared model-utils file; had to determine helpers.js was the right home by grepping for common imports.
open_items: Model sort logic is still duplicated inline across three files rather than using a single shared sort function from helpers.js — could be further consolidated.
Version extraction from model IDs is heuristic-based; edge cases with unusual model ID formats may not sort correctly.
